title_001: EQ Emotional Intelligence Test — Dopamine Lab
meta_001: EQ Emotional Intelligence Test — a 4-part read on how you handle your feelings
meta_002: Self-awareness, self-control, empathy, expression. 16 questions to map your emotional-intelligence profile.
html_004: If IQ is the power to solve problems,
html_008: 16 questions to diagnose your 4 emotional skills.
html_010: Noticing your feelings · Steadying them · Reading others · Speaking your heart.
html_011: There are no wrong answers — the more you answer as
html_015:
※ A self-report reference test, not a clinical assessment.
    EQ isn’t something you’re born with — it grows with practice, so your results come with growth pointers too.
html_016: Four emotional skills stack up in real time — the full picture unlocks at the end!
html_017: 👉 Answer as the ‘real you’, not the ‘you you wish you were’!
html_021: ※ EQ is like a muscle — just two weeks of tending your lowest axis can shift it.
html_022: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_001: When your mood dips for no clear reason, you…
js_002: Pause and ask yourself ‘why?’ to trace the reason
js_003: Just do something else to shake the mood off
js_005: You can describe how you’re doing pretty specifically
js_006: “Oh, you know, same as always” comes out first
js_008: You can see the real feeling under it (hurt, worry)
js_009: You’re just angry — the reason can wait
js_010: When your eyes well up over something small…
js_011: You catch it: ‘ah, I’ve been worn out lately’
js_012: You brush it off as ‘random’ and move on
js_013: When a sharp word rises to the tip of your tongue…
js_014: You take a beat and pick your words before letting them out
js_015: You say it on the spot, then think
js_017: You have a reliable routine that resets you
js_018: You just wing it, however the mood goes
js_020: You focus on the one thing you can do right now
js_021: Worst-case scenarios auto-play in your head
js_022: When the day ends with you still upset…
js_023: You sort your feelings out for tomorrow-you, then sleep
js_024: You sleep on it and wake up still in a bad mood
js_025: When a friend opens up about a worry…
js_026: You read the feeling first, before any fix
js_027: You jump straight to the fastest solution
js_028: When you watch a show or movie, you…
js_029: You cry and laugh right along with the characters
js_030: You analyze the plot and setup as you watch
js_031: When the person with you looks down…
js_032: You usually notice before they say a word
js_033: You only catch on once they tell you
js_034: When you hear the exact opposite opinion…
js_035: ‘From their side, that makes sense’ comes to mind first
js_036: A counter-argument pops up automatically first
js_037: When you feel grateful or sorry…
js_038: You put it into words in the moment
js_039: You tend to keep it to yourself
js_040: When friction shows up in a relationship…
js_041: You ask to talk before feelings get hurt
js_042: You wait for time to sort it out
js_044: You open up honestly to someone you trust
js_046: When you get a compliment, you…
js_047: You take it: “Thank you, that means a lot”
js_048: “Oh no, not at all” slips out first
js_050: The power to notice your own feelings
js_051: Once a day, ask yourself: ‘my mood right now, in one word?’ The moment you name a feeling, half of it is already handled.
js_053: The power to steady emotions instead of being swept up
js_054: Set one ‘3-second routine’ for when you snap (a breath, a sip of water). Control isn’t willpower — it’s routine.
js_056: The power to read what others feel
js_057: In a conversation, lead with ‘that must’ve been hard’ before any advice. Empathy isn’t a gift — it’s a matter of order.
js_059: The power to let feelings out in a healthy way
js_060: Text one person you’re grateful for today and tell them so. The expression muscle grows from short sentences first.
js_062: All four skills have ripened evenly. There’s a reason people feel safe around you.
js_064: You’ve got a sure hand with feelings! Polish your one lowest axis and you’re the complete package.
js_066: Skills you’re good at live side by side with ones you’re not. Your weak axis isn’t a flaw — it’s room to grow.
js_068: You and your emotions are still a little unfamiliar. That’s okay — EQ is a skill you grow from here.
js_070: You haven’t opened the box of your heart in a while. May today’s result be the first key to it.
js_073: ) is your steadiest — you’re already using this one well.
